Petition For Authority To Refinance Reverse Mortgage
Off Calendar Feb-01-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nancy Rasch for Debra Dolch (Trustee).

The Court expressed concern about how high the expense is for the proposed refinance of the reverse mortgage stating that it has a high interest rate and high fees involved, and questions whether or not there are any other options. Ms. Rasch stated that there are no other alternatives, that it is clear in the trust document that Mr. Tooke wants to remain in his home as long as he is physically and financially able, and that there is only six months left on the reverse mortgage. Ms. Rasch also stated that it is very expensive to keep someone at home, that if they move Mr. Tooke they will also displace the Daughter, that Mr. Tooke is 92 years old, and that if they withdraw the lump sum, the amount of interest earned if placed in a financial institution is probably not going to be the amount they are paying. The Court granted the Petition for Authority to Refinance Reverse Mortgage (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Dec-07-2016 Off Calendar

Petition For Authority To Enter Into Reverse Mortgage, For Waiver Of Accounting, For Allowance Of Compensation To Trustee And Attorney And For Fees On Account To Trustee
Off Calendar Dec-05-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch for (trustee) Debra Dolch. Endy Ukoha-Ajike (via court call) for Thalia Tooke. Court Appointed Counsel Franchesca Callejo for (settlor) Theodore Tooke.

Ms. Callejo reported that she interviewed Mr. Tooke, that his health has improved, that it is clear that he wants to be home and that the property was in a horrible condition but has been cleaned up. Ms. Rasch stated that Mr. Tooke has the right to the least restrictive home and that his health and the conditions at home have improved. The Court asked Mr. Ajike about Ms. Tooke paying rent. Mr. Ajike stated that Ms. Tooke has no objections to him coming home, that they want a plan laid out, that he needs 24 hour care and that the response clarified that he will have it. The Court asked about the payment/non-payment of rent. Mr. Ajike stated that they have no objections, that her father never charged her rent, that they had an arrangement and that her father would not want to charge her rent. Ms. Rasch stated that it was his practice to support Ms. Tooke, that she used money with his consent, that if funds do run out her paying rent would help, that there is an issue with rent control and that they do not want to address the rent issues at this time. Ms. Callejo confirmed that Mr. Tooke and his daughter are close. The Petition for Authority to Enter into Reverse Mortgage, etc., was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Nov-25-2015 Off Calendar

Petition For Order Removing T. Tooke As Co-Trustee, Appointing D.J. Dolch As Successor Trustee And Bringing Trutst Under Court Supervision.
Off Calendar Sep-02-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch for and with Debra Dolch. Endy Ukoha-Ajike for Thalia Tooke. Court appointed counsel Francesca Callejo for Theodore Tooke.

Ms. Rasch reported that there is a companion conservatorship, that it has been contested, that Carolyn Martin and Debra Dolch have been appointed conservators, that they are requesting that Ms. Dolch be appointed successor trustee, that they feel it is in the best interest of Mr. Tooke, that it would be unworkable to have a 3rd person involved, that Mr. Tooke wants to move back home and that a reverse mortgage will be needed, that they had a productive meeting last week in which the parties agreed to work together but that it has not been tried yet, that they presented the idea of Ms. Dolch serving as trustee would cease upon the death of Mr. Tooke but have not heard from counsel and that Anita Charles was served and appeared at the temporary conservatorship hearing but has not been heard from since. Mr. Ukoha-Ajike stated that he submitted a brief, that the trust instructions are clear as to how Mr. Tooke wants a successor trustee appointed, that there is a willingness to work together, that they do not agree with the appointment of successor trustee and that it oversteps the powers of the court. Ms. Callejo reported that Mr. Tooke has confidence in his daughter, that his level of comprehension is not clear, that a lot of work needs to be done before he can return home, that she is conflicted with the proposal, that she is not sure Ms. Tooke can be bonded, has concerns that prior to last Friday they had not heard anything from Ms. Tooke, that there has been no response to her question as to what is needed, that Ms. Tooke will need a tremendous amount of attorney support and guidance whereas Ms. Dolch would not and that the court would have to remove Ms. Tooke as trustee. Mr. Ukoha-Ajike stated that if Ms. Tooke does not remain as trustee that they would like to nominate Herb Thomas. Ms. Callejo stated that Mr. Thomas is reputable, that it is her understanding that he is not taking on conservatorship cases at this time, that he is trying to avoid stressful assignments and that the would need a commitment from him. Ms. Rasch stated that if they were to choose a professional other than Ms. Dolch they would want them to step in as conservator and trustee to avoid adding a third person. The Court requested that the parties submit proposed orders and that Mr. Ukoha-Ajike is to provide some sort of analysis as to Herb Thomas accepting the appointment. The petition for order removing T. Tooke as co-trustee, appointing Debra Dolch as successor trustee, etc., was taken under submission.

Judge: Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
